title: [BUG] When using the addImage method and specifying the cell width with tl and br after changing the cell width, the intended width is not set.
author: RyokoKassai

comments.author: wwwxy80s
comments.body:
I read the source code of exceljs and consulted some information. I found a way to accurately add images through the br attribute, but I don’t know if it is universal. I would like to share some information I found.

When calling addImage using the br attribute, you need to determine the two anchor points of the upper left corner and the lower right corner of the image.

Take a single anchor point br (bottomRight) as an example. In addition to col and row attributes, you also need to pass nativeCol, nativeRow, nativeColOff, and nativeRowOff (all must be passed. I tested that after passing these four values, col and row parameters can be omitted). nativeRowOff and nativeColOff represent the offset size of the anchor point in the cell where it is located. The unit is EMUs. nativeCol and nativeRow represent the row and column of the cell where it is located. The value is an integer.

Then there is the following conversion formula

1. 1EMU =1 / 914400 inch
2. 1 inch = 96 pixels (assuming the display DPI is 96)
3. So 1 pixel = 914400 / 96EMU
4. ExcelJsColWidthInEMU = ExcelJsColWidth * 10000
5. ExcelJsrowHeightInEMU = ExcelJsRowHeight * 10000

In my case,I calculate the Image pixel offset  in bottom right cell
Here is the code

``` typescript
// The pixel offset of the lower right corner of the image in the cell where it is located
const ImageWidthPixelOffsetInBottomRightCell = 20
const bottomRightCellWidth = 32
const bottomRightCellWidthInEmu = bottomRightCellWidth * 10000
// index of the cell where the bottom right corner of the image is located (starting from 0, integer value)
const nativeRow = 3
const imageWidthEMUOffsetInBottomRightCell =  ImageWidthPixelOffsetInBottomRightCell * 914400 / 96
const nativeRowOff = ImageWidthEMUOffsetInBottomRightCell
const row = nativeRow + nativeRowOff/bottomRightCellWidthInEmu
```

The calculation of column-related parameters is similar
The above calculation method is implemented by referring to the calculation logic in the exceljs source code, removing some fault-tolerant logic. If possible, it is recommended to look at the code in the following file
https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/5bed18b45e824f409b08456b59b87430ded023ab/lib/doc/anchor.js

comments.author: wwwxy80s
comments.body:
In your case
calculate params in br
I don't know if it works, you can try it
```typescript
const bottomRightCellWidth = 32
const bottomRightCellWidthInEmu = bottomRightCellWidth * 10000
const nativeRowOff = 0.8 * bottomRightCellWidthInEmu
const nativeRow = 3
const row = 3.8

// maybe different,my excel default font is chinese font
const bottomRightRowHeight = 16.8
const bottomRightRowHeightInEmu = bottomRightRowHeight * 10000
const nativeColOff = 0.5 * bottomRightRowHeightInEmu
const nativeCol = 3
const col = 3.5

```

comments.author: ff-stan
comments.body:
> 在你的情况 下计算参数 我不知道它是否有效，你可以尝试一下
> 
> ```ts
> const bottomRightCellWidth = 32
> const bottomRightCellWidthInEmu = bottomRightCellWidth * 10000
> const nativeRowOff = 0.8 * bottomRightCellWidthInEmu
> const nativeRow = 3
> const row = 3.8
> 
> // maybe different,my excel default font is chinese font
> const bottomRightRowHeight = 16.8
> const bottomRightRowHeightInEmu = bottomRightRowHeight * 10000
> const nativeColOff = 0.5 * bottomRightRowHeightInEmu
> const nativeCol = 3
> const col = 3.5
> ```
This is very useful to me, thank you！！！
